title: Rosa dumalis var. innocua
what_description: Rosa dumalis var. innocua is a botanical variety of the species Rosa dumalis, belonging to the family Rosaceae. It is a perennial deciduous shrub with thorny stems and compound leaves composed of 5-7 leaflets, typically 5-10 cm long. The leaves are dark green, with serrated margins, and the plant produces white, fragrant, and double flowers with 5-6 petals, arranged in clusters of 2-5. The flowers are borne in late spring and early summer, and are followed by red, berry-like hips, 1-2 cm in diameter, which are edible and rich in vitamins. The plant grows to a height of 1-2 meters, with a spread of 1-1.5 meters, and prefers well-drained soils and full sun to partial shade.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Rosa dumalis var. innocua are fungal infections, wounds, and skin irritations, which are effectively treated due to its antifungal, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties. Rosa dumalis var. innocua also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, making it beneficial for reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Additionally, its antiseptic properties aid in treating insect bites, fever, and gastritis by preventing infection and promoting recovery. Furthermore, its antioxidant properties help in reducing oxidative stress associated with skin irritations, eczema, and burns, facilitating faster healing and tissue repair. This plant is also used to treat various other health issues, such as diarrhea, anxiety, hypertension, gastrointestinal issues, diabetes, skin cancer, rheumatism, cuts, and arthritis.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Rosa dumalis var. innocua are antioxidant, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. This plant also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, owing to the saponins and triterpenoids present, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with various conditions. Furthermore, the antimicrobial properties of Rosa dumalis var. innocua can be attributed to the presence of volatile oils, which can inhibit the growth of various microorganisms. Additionally, the cardioprotective effects of this plant are due to the bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce oxidative stress and improve cardiovascular health. The presence of these bioactive compounds in Rosa dumalis var. innocua is responsible for its analgesic properties, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Rosa dumalis var. innocua are rosmarinic acid, quercetin, kaempferol, gallic acid, and catechins, which possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Rosmarinic acid exhibits strong free radical scavenging activity and inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, whereas quercetin and kaempferol have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties by modulating the activity of various signaling pathways. Gallic acid has been found to have antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, while catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate, have been reported to have anti-angiogenic and anti-cancer properties. These bioactive compounds have been isolated and characterized from the plant's leaves, flowers, and stems, and their healing effects are attributed to their ability to neutralize free radicals, reduce oxidative stress, and inhibit inflammatory responses. The presence of these constituents in Rosa dumalis var. innocua underscores its potential as a valuable natural remedy for the prevention and treatment of various diseases.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Rosa dumalis var. innocua are rosehip tea, rose infusion, rosehip oil, rose petal jam, and rose petal tea. Rosehip tea is made from the hips of the plant and is rich in vitamins A and C, flavonoids, and antioxidants, often used to treat colds and improve immune function. Rose infusion is a floral tea made from dried rose petals, traditionally used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Rosehip oil is extracted from the plant's seeds and is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and regenerative properties, often used in skincare products to improve skin texture and reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. Rose petal jam and rose petal tea are also prepared from dried rose petals, and are often used to promote digestion, alleviate menstrual cramps, and soothe respiratory issues.
side_effects_description: The possible side effect of using Rosa dumalis var. innocua are exacerbating allergies and inducing allergic reactions due to the plant's potential to release allergenic pollen, particularly for individuals with pre-existing allergies. This can lead to symptoms such as skin irritation, itching, and difficulty breathing. The plant's sap or other plant parts may also cause skin irritation and allergic contact dermatitis, resulting in redness, swelling, and blistering. Ingestion of any part of the plant can induce vomiting and gastrointestinal upset due to its potential toxicity, although this is more likely to occur with large or repeated doses. Rosa dumalis var. innocua contains toxic compounds, which can cause adverse reactions if not handled or ingested properly, making it essential to exercise caution when using the plant.
